div.h2: Savi Vision Private Limited — Odoo 17 Community Edition

div.p: The payroll system is built on the Cybrosys HR Payroll Community module (since Odoo 17 CE does not include native payroll). On top of it, several extension modules provide accounting integration, automation, dashboards, reporting, and Indian compliance features.

tr.td: Employee contracts with salary and duration
Indian compliance fields (PAN, Aadhar, PF, ESIC, etc.)

div.div: System Administrator (base.group_system) | v Payroll Manager (group_hr_payroll_community_manager) | - Inherits all Officer permissions | - Unrestricted payslip access | - Batch payslip management | - Configuration access v Payroll Officer (group_hr_payroll_community_user) | - Inherits HR User + Contract Manager | - Department-restricted payslip access | - Salary rule & structure management v HR User (hr.group_hr_user) | - Read-only access to salary structures | - Employee and contract viewing v Internal User (base.group_user) - No payroll access - Basic employee self-service only
div.p:
Who should have this: IT Admin, System Owner
Purpose: Full system configuration, module installation, technical settings

Who should have this: HR Head, Finance Manager, or designated Payroll Manager
Purpose: Full payroll processing, batch management, configuration, reporting
Key privilege: Can see ALL payslips across ALL departments (no record restrictions)

Who should have this: HR executives, payroll processors
Purpose: Day-to-day payslip creation and processing
Key restriction: Can only see payslips for their own employee record, employees in departments they manage, and employees with no department assigned.

Who should have this: Any staff who needs to view employee info
Payroll access: Can only view salary structures and contract templates (read-only)

Who should have this: All internal staff
Payroll access: None
div.div: Note: Currently all 11 payroll users have BOTH Officer and Manager roles. This should be reviewed — service engineers, electricians, and coordinators likely should NOT have payroll access.

ol.li: Go to Payroll > Payslips > All Payslips
Click Compute Sheet to calculate salary lines
Review the computed lines (Basic, Allowances, Deductions, Net)
Click Generate to create payslips for all selected employees
Click Close to finalize the batch
Go to Payroll > Configuration > Salary Structures
Go to Payroll > Configuration > Salary Rules
Each rule has: Name, Code, Category, Sequence, Computation Type
Computation types: Fixed Amount, Percentage, Python Code

div.p: Automatically creates accounting journal entries when payslips are confirmed.

ol.li: Each salary rule is mapped to a Debit Account and Credit Account
Each contract has a Salary Journal assigned
When a payslip moves to "Done" state, a journal entry is automatically created
If totals don't balance, an adjustment entry is added

div.h4: Setting Up Accounts on Salary Rules

ol.li: Go to Payroll > Configuration > Salary Rules
Under Accounting tab, set Debit Account, Credit Account, and optionally Analytic Account

div.p: Automatically generates payslip batches on a schedule via a daily cron job.

ol.li: Choose generation option: First of Month, End of Month, or Specific Date

div.div: Note: Payslips are created in Draft state — they still need manual confirmation.

tr.td: Employee count, own attendance, leave balance, work days
Payslip stats, department-wise leave analytics, attendance across teams, expense summaries

ol.li: Go to Payroll > Reporting > Payroll Analysis
Filter by date range, employee, department, salary rule, structure, or state
Group by: Employee, Department, Job Position, Rule Category
Go to Payroll > Payslips > All Payslips
Review and send using the email composer
Enable Automatic Send Payslip By Mail

ul.li: Review and clean up user roles (see Section 3.3 recommendations)
Set up departments properly (remove duplicates)
Verify job positions are correctly assigned to employees
Fill in Indian compliance fields for all 22 employees (PAN, Aadhar, UAN, ESIC)
Create contracts for all 21 employees who currently lack contracts
Configure Basic Wage and all applicable allowances
Configure all applicable deductions (PF, ESIC, Professional Tax, etc.)
Assign Salary Structure to each contract
Set contract start date and type (Running/Open)
Set Salary Journal for accounting integration
Clean up duplicate structures (BASE, SAVI DELHI, SALARY STRUCTURE)
Design salary structures for different employee categories
Create salary rules: Basic, HRA, Conveyance, Special Allowance, etc.
Create deduction rules: PF (12%), ESIC (0.75%), Professional Tax, Income Tax
Map GL accounts to salary rules (Debit/Credit accounts)
Set up Contribution Registers for PF, ESIC, PT
Paid Time Off (requires allocation)
Sick Time Off (no allocation needed)
Unpaid Leave
Compensatory Days (requires allocation)
Create leave allocations for eligible employees
Verify leave approval workflows match company policy
Configure automatic payroll generation timing
Configure email payslip distribution
Test payroll dashboard
Process a test payslip for one employee
Verify salary computation matches expected CTC breakdown
Verify accounting entries are created correctly
Test batch payslip generation
Review payslip report output (PDF)
Test email distribution of payslips
Process first full payroll cycle
All 7 payroll modules are installed and active
22 employees are registered with departments and job positions
4 leave types are configured
Basic salary structures exist
Payroll dashboard is available

td.strong: No GL accounts mapped to salary rules
All payroll users have Manager role

tr.td: PAN, Aadhar, UAN, ESIC not filled

td.strong: All 3 existing payslips are Draft with 0 amounts

tr.td: Test data, should be cleaned up

div.p:
HR & Payroll User Manual v1.0 — Savi Vision Private Limited
Prepared by Macrofix Implementation Team — February 2026
